serviceDescription.aws: AWS Fargate is a serverless compute engine for containers that works with both Amazon Elastic Container Service (ECS) and Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS). Fargate makes it easy for you to focus on building your applications. Fargate removes the need to provision and manage servers, lets you specify and pay for resources per application, and improves security through application isolation by design.

helpText.aws: AWS Fargate pricing is calculated based on the vCPU and memory resources used from the time you start to download your container image until the Amazon ECS Task or Amazon EKS Pod terminates, rounded up to the nearest second. Pricing is per second with a 1-minute minimum. Duration is calculated from the time you start to download your container image (docker pull) until the Task or Pod terminates, rounded up to the nearest second.

components.label: info alert
components.content: Fargate Spot and Compute Savings Plans are currently not supported on the AWS Pricing Calculator.

components.text: With AWS Fargate, there are no upfront payments and you only pay for the resources that you use. Pricing is based on requested vCPU, memory, and storage resources consumed by your Amazon ECS Task or Amazon EKS Pod.
With Amazon Fargate, there are no upfront payments and you only pay for the resources that you use. Pricing is based on requested vCPU and memory resources consumed by your Amazon ECS Task.

components.label: Number of tasks or pods
components.description: Enter the number of tasks or pods running for your application

components.label: Average duration
components.placeholder: Enter the average duration for each task
components.description: Enter the time period for which your tasks or pods are running. Pricing is per second with a 1-minute minimum (Linux) and a 5-minute minimum (Windows). Duration is calculated from the time you start to download your container image (docker pull) until the Task or Pod terminates, rounded up to the nearest second.

components.label: Amount of vCPU allocated
components.description: Enter the amount between 0.25 vCPU and 16 vCPU

components.content: vCPU selected supports memory values 0.5 GB, 1 GB, or 2 GB

components.content: vCPU selected supports memory values between 1 GB and 4 GB, in 1 GB increments

components.content: vCPU selected supports memory values between 4 GB and 16 GB, in 1 GB increments

components.content: vCPU selected supports memory values between 8 GB and 30 GB, in 1 GB increments

components.label: Amount of ephemeral storage allocated for Amazon ECS
components.description: Enter the amount between 20 GB and 200 GB. The first 20 GB are at no additional charge, you only pay for any additional storage that you configure for the Task.
